title: Phlomoides fimbriata
taxonomy: Phlomoides fimbriata is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Lamiaceae. It belongs to the genus Phlomoides and is native to Europe and Asia. The exact classification is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Eudicots, Clade: Asterids, Order: Lamiales, Family: Lamiaceae, Genus: Phlomoides, Species: P. fimbriata. Phlomoides fimbriata is closely related to other species within the same genus, such as Phlomoides purpurea and Phlomoides tuberosa. Further research may provide more detailed classification information.

intro: Phlomoides fimbriata, also known as Fimbriated Phlomis or Jerusalem Sage, is a perennial plant species in the mint family (Lamiaceae) native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and exhibit antimicrobial and antioxidant activities, which contribute to its health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Phlomoides fimbriata include phenolic acids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Phlomoides fimbriata is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also used in the production of herbal supplements, such as capsules and tablets.
